name: Mississippi (United States, 1894-2020)
description: Three horizontal stripes of equal width — blue, white, red — covering the field, with the Confederate Battle Flag (red field, blue saltire fimbriated white, with thirteen white five-pointed stars) in the upper hoist canton.
history: Adopted on 7 February 1894 by the Mississippi Legislature, replacing the 'Magnolia Flag' of 1861. The design combined the bars of the first Confederate national flag with the Confederate Battle Flag in the canton. After the original adopting statute was inadvertently repealed in the 1906 code revision, the flag's official status remained unclear until a 2001 referendum, in which Mississippians voted nearly two-to-one to retain the design over a proposed alternative without the Confederate symbol. Following the murder of George Floyd and broader protests over Confederate iconography in 2020, the Mississippi Legislature voted on 28 June 2020 to retire the flag; Governor Tate Reeves signed the repeal on 30 June 2020. A new flag (the Magnolia Flag) was adopted in 2021. Mississippi was the last U.S. state to display the Confederate battle emblem on its flag.

features.symbolism: the colours of the United States and the Confederate States
the Confederate Battle Flag, representing Mississippi's Confederate heritage
the Southern Cross of the Confederate Battle Flag

features.role: Stars of the Confederate Battle Flag
features.symbolism: the eleven seceding Confederate states plus Missouri and Kentucky
